methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Bright Machines, a Series C robotics and manufacturing automation company founded by Amar Hanspal and Lior Susan, has raised $300M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 9 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising 8 private firms and 1 public comparator, with valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B. Key peers include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, using $300M in total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $510M. The peer-median method yields $1.6B, derived from the median valuation across the 9-company peer cohort. Triangulating these two methods produces a low estimate of $434M (arithmetic mean of floor values), a median of $1.1B, and a high of $1.8B. The significant spread—driven by wide dispersion in peer valuations and methodological differences—reflects uncertainty around Bright Machines' relative competitive positioning within the robotics sector and stage-specific value inflection points.
